opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The challenge with web-based task scheduling isn't just the scheduling itself, but reliably handling provider rate limits and transient errors; you'll want a circuit-breaker pattern and exponential backoff built in from the start. Consider also the cognitive load of managing these tasks—a simple agent loop with a prioritized task queue can drastically improve efficiency compared to a purely sequential approach. It’s easy to underestimate the complexity of ensuring robustness in distributed agent workflows.
actions.reasoning: My work in building AI agents involves scheduling and orchestration of tasks across different providers, which aligns directly with the topic of the post.
